items.title: What we know about new MACC chief Abdul Halim Aman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — After the announcement of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new Chief Commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), Malaysians will likely be curious about Abdul Halim’s background. Abdul Halim brings to his new role decades of judicial experience. A former High Court judge, Abdul Halim retired from the bench in 2023 after nearly two decades of service.

items.title: Anwar: RTD's digital shift not just an upgrade, but a 'necessity' for integrity
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: The Road Transport Department (RTD) must spearhead digital reform as part of broader efforts to strengthen integrity, reduce public friction and meet rising expectations for public service delivery, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the agency's frontline enforcement role made it one of the most exposed government bodies, where every decision is scrutinised by the public — particularly in the era of social media. Anwar said rising public expectations, amplified by digital connectivity, meant government agencies must adapt quickly and responsibly.

items.title: Pas hopes new MACC chief's appointment will lead to significant reforms
items.summary: The party's information chief, Ahmad Fadhli Shaari, said this was important in ensuring the agency remains respected and independent. The Pasir Mas member of parliament also listed several cases for the next MACC chief, Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman, to tackle, including allegations concerning the existence of a "corporate mafia". Other calls included an investigation into the share ownership of current chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki.

items.title: GOF detain fisherman for alleged diesel subsidy misuse
items.summary: YAN: A man was detained by the General Operations Force (GOF) while filling drums with fuel at a petrol station here yesterday. He is believed to be a part-time fisherman and is alleged to have used a subsidised diesel fleet card belonging to a family member to purchase diesel at the station. The Kedah Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) said in a statement that enforcement officers from KPDN Alor Setar took custody of the suspect after his arrest by the GOF at the location.

items.title: RON95 sales at border stations drop 20pct following police, GOF deployment
items.summary: LANGKAWI: Sales of RON95 petrol have dropped by 20 per cent following the deployment of police and General Operations Force (GOF) personnel at 170 high-risk stations near the border. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said that based on records from April 13 to 21, RON95 sales showed a significant decline, while RON97 sales recorded an increase. "Among the items seized were diesel, petrol, liquefied petroleum gas (LPG), cooking oil, coarse sugar, and wheat flour, reflecting the variety of commodities targeted by smuggling activities," he said.

items.title: Heed PM's call, allow WFH, Ramanan tells bosses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Private sector employers have been urged to heed Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's call to adopt flexible work arrangements, including work-from-home (WFH). Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said many large corporations, multinationals, and banks have already adopted WFH practices. "The call to consider flexible work arrangements and WFH practices should be implemented by private companies where possible.

items.title: Family hopes for miracle after Malaysian jailed in Japan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Muhammad Faiq Zafran Mohd Jailani, 24, who is currently detained in Toyamanishi, Japan, was sentenced to four years' imprisonment by the Toyama District Court yesterday. She said this means Faiq's sentence has been reduced from four years to three years and nine months. Yesterday, Noor Svetlana and Faiq's father, Mohd Jailani, travelled to Japan for the second time to attend the hearing and sentencing.

items.title: US envoys to head to Pakistan Saturday for Iran talks
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will head on Saturday to Pakistan for a new round of talks with Iran on ending the war, the White House said. "I can confirm Special Envoy Witkoff and Jared Kushner will be off to Pakistan again tomorrow morning to engage in talks," White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt told Fox News on Friday. The talks would come more than two weeks after Vice President JD Vance held a first round of talks with Iranian representatives in Pakistan.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Crucial to maintain good ties with major powers amid global energy crisis
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said it makes "a lot of sense" for Malaysia to continue establishing and maintaining good relations with other countries as global tensions threaten to disrupt economic stability and energy supplies. Anwar said Malaysia's ability to maintain ties with major powers, Gulf states and Iran is critical as the conflict involving Israel, the United States and Iran intensifies, with spillover effects already impacting the wider Middle East and global markets. However, Anwar said that the ongoing conflict is beginning to affect global supply chains, particularly fuel, with oil prices rising and diesel shortages reported in several countries.

items.title: Padini says it is unaware of wrongdoing as MACC freezes accounts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Garment retailer Padini Holdings Bhd says it is not aware of any allegations against the company following the freezing of certain bank accounts linked to an ongoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) investigation involving external counterparties. Padini said certain identified bank accounts of the company and its subsidiaries were frozen on April 24 pursuant to an order issued under Section 44(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities (AMLA) Act 2001. Yesterday, Padini said in a Bursa Malaysia filing that the MACC had frozen certain bank accounts of Padini Holdings Bhd and its subsidiaries in connection with an ongoing investigation.

items.title: RoS mulls independent audit mechanism for NGOs collecting donations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Registrar of Societies (RoS) is studying proposals for guidelines and independent audits for organisations collecting public funds, following reports of alleged misappropriation involving hundreds of millions by two NGOs. Director-general Datuk Mohd Zulfikar Ahmad said the reports have raised concerns over the transparency in the management of donations. This week, the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) uncovered two separate NGOs involved in the misappropriation of funds.

items.title: Anwar floats diesel road tax cut to offset high pump prices
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed the Transport Ministry to study the possibility of reducing road tax for diesel vehicles, offering a potential relief measure for owners grappling with soaring fuel prices that the government says it cannot afford to lower directly. The move comes after diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia surged dramatically, jumping from around RM3 per litre in late February to over RM5.50 per litre by late March, following adjustments to the government's subsidy mechanism. In Malaysia, road tax for diesel vehicles is generally higher than for petrol vehicles of the same engine capacity due to an additional diesel surcharge.

items.title: Only 2pct of gig workers covered: Socso warns platforms to protect workers now
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Social Security Organisation (Socso) has told platform providers to promptly align their systems and submit details of their workers to comply with the Gig Workers Act (Act 872). It said the move is not merely a regulatory requirement, but a critical step to ensure consistent and continuous social protection for gig workers. "Gig workers are constantly exposed to risks while earning a living.

items.title: Factory worker wins electric motorcycle in lucky draw
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: A customer service worker won a new YADEA RS20 electric motorcycle after a lucky draw at Karnival Jom Heboh X Mega JPJ Madani 2026 in Batu Kawan. Adriana Athirah Abdul Wahab, 23, from Nibong Tebal, did not expect her name to be announced as the grand prize winner after attending the Battle of the Band competition at Penang State Stadium last night. "We never thought we would actually win the lucky draw for the grand prize.

items.title: Record bids for "P1P" plate as tender raises RM21.6mil
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: "P1P" registration number fetched RM1.1 million in a special tender, as the "P_P" series exercise raised RM21.6 million and drew strong public participation. Transport Minister Anthony Loke Siew Fook said the series, introduced in conjunction with the Road Transport Department's (RTD) 80th anniversary, drew participation via the JPJeBid system, reflecting public confidence in its transparent, fully digital tender process. "In total, the exercise raised RM21.68 million, comprising RM19.19 million from bids and RM2.48 million in service fees," he said at the RTD 80th anniversary celebration today.

items.title: Technical hurdles slow down gig platform registration with Perkeso
items.summary: LETTERS: Less than one per cent of gig platforms have pre-registered with the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso), and only two platforms across the industry are fully compliant with application programming interface (API) integration requirements. My Mobility Vision (MMV), a Malaysian transport and mobility policy organisation, said it convened the MMV Gig Economy Roundtable in Cyberjaya in March, bringing together operators, regulators and legal counsel to assess the implementation of Act 872. It said discussions and subsequent integration sessions with Perkeso revealed that platforms were not unwilling to comply, but were facing incomplete systems and requirements.

items.title: Sabah BN unveils new leadership line-up for 2026
items.summary: Arthur, who is also Parti Bersatu Rakyat Sabah president, remains chairman, while Sabah Umno chief Datuk Jafry Ariffin has been appointed deputy chairman. Three vice-chairmen were named: Datuk Richard Kastum, Datuk Teah Heok Kuin and Chandrra Segar Soosaimany. Datuk Mohd Hasnol Ayub was appointed secretary, Datuk Mohamad Alamin as treasurer, and Datuk Abdul Aziz Julkarnain as strategic communications director.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Govt considers road tax cut for diesel vehicles amid cost pressure
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: The government is considering reducing road tax for diesel vehicles to cushion the impact of rising fuel costs, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said diesel vehicles account for about 1.8 million units nationwide — a relatively small share of the total vehicle population — but acknowledged that owners are feeling the strain from higher diesel prices. "There are about 1.8 million diesel vehicles.

items.title: From assistant officer to chief commissioner: The 40-year journey of Azam Baki
items.summary: He was appointed the sixth chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) on March 9, 2020, succeeding Latheefa Koya, who resigned on March 6. Azam, 63, began his journey in public service in 1984 when he joined the Anti-Corruption Agency (now the MACC) as an assistant investigation officer. He held several key leadership roles within the MACC, including Intelligence Division director (2013), Investigation Division director (2015), and deputy chief commissioner (Operations) (2016).

items.title: Guan Eng backs Selangor DAP rep over pig farming row, accuses PAS of ‘double standards’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — DAP advisor Lim Guan Eng has responded to critics of Seri Kembangan assemblyman Wong Siew Ki, accusing PAS of applying “double standards” for labelling her “treasonous” over her suggestion to review state pig farming policies. “If PAS is consistent and responsible, they should have taken action against the PAS minister who was reprimanded by the King,” Lim said. He also expressed regret that Umno had joined PAS in criticising Wong.

items.title: New MACC chief appointment a test for the system, says watchdog
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Public confidence in the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will hinge on its new leadership's handling of high-profile cases, says an anti-graft watchdog. Malaysia Corruption Watch (MCW) president Jais Abdul Karim said the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman is a litmus test for the nation's institutional framework rather than a mere change in leadership. "Public confidence will only be restored if this new leadership can act decisively on high-profile cases without fear or favour," he said in a statement.

items.title: Iran's foreign minister arrives in Pakistan, raising hopes for US peace talks
items.summary: ISLAMABAD: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi arrived in the Pakistani capital Islamabad on Friday to discuss proposals for restarting peace talks with the United States, amid conflicting signals on whether he would meet with US negotiators there. Pakistani sources said Araqchi was not slated to meet US negotiators in Islamabad, while White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner would leave for Pakistan on Saturday morning for talks with the Iranian official. Pakistan's foreign minister confirmed the arrival of the Iranian delegation, saying Araqchi would meet with senior Pakistani officials to discuss the peace efforts.

items.title: Woman arrested for allegedly sparking bushfire through open burning
items.summary: BACHOK: Police have arrested a woman suspected of causing a bushfire at Jalan Pantai Perkasa here yesterday. District police chief Superintendent Mohamad Ismail Jamaluddin said police were alerted to the incident at 4.30pm. Ismail said the affected area covered open land between the abandoned house and the front of the complainant's residence.

items.title: Padini says MACC probe targets ‘external parties,’ not company itself
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Padini Holdings Berhad has clarified that the recent freezing of its bank accounts by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is related to an investigation involving its “external counterparties,” and not the company or its management. The statement follows Padini’s official filing with Bursa Malaysia yesterday, which first confirmed that several of its bank accounts had been frozen under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA). Padini’s statement today specifies that the investigation targets certain external parties who are “not employees, officers, or part of the group’s management.” “The company wishes to emphasise that, based on information currently available, we are not aware of any allegation or wrongdoing on our part and understand that the freezing order is a procedure taken in the course of the investigation,” Padini said.

items.title: King consents to Abdul Halim's appointment as new MACC chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has consented to the appointment of former High Court judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), effective May 13. Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar said the appointment is in line with Subsection 5(1) of the MACC Act 2009 (Act 694). Abdul Halim's appointment will replace Tan Sri Azam Baki, whose tenure is scheduled to end on May 12.

items.title: Organisers say rally against Azam Baki to proceed despite announcement of new MACC chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — The “Tangkap Azam Baki” rally calling for action against outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief Tan Sri Azam Baki will still proceed this afternoon despite his named replacement, its organisers said today. “The rally will proceed as planned, and our three demands still stand. Earlier today, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar announced that the new MACC chief from May 13 onwards would be Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman.

items.title: Poodle abuse: Animal welfare group calls for help to catch suspect
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Stray Animal Association of Malaysia (SAFM) has condemned the viral abuse of a poodle in Seri Kembangan, calling on members of the public with information on the person responsible to assist investigators immediately. "Animals also feel pain, fear, and trauma," SAFM said in a post on Facebook yesterday. Members of the public can also channel information directly to SAFM at 013-706 6770.

items.title: Elderly man flung off bike, killed after two motorcycles collide
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: An elderly man was killed and two others injured when two motorcycles collided at Sungai Kamin, Hulu Selangor, last night. Hulu Selangor police chief Superintendent Ibrahim Husin said the crash, which occurred shortly before midnight, involved a Honda EX-5 and a Yamaha Ego. The impact flung the elderly man and his motorcycle onto the road shoulder.

items.title: Bomba deploys helicopter to water-bomb Dungun peat fire as big as housing estate
items.summary: DUNGUN, April 25 — The Fire and Rescue Department has deployed a helicopter to assist in extinguishing a peat swamp forest fire that has been spreading towards residential areas in Kampung Baru, Kuala Abang, near here since yesterday. Terengganu Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Izwan Hashim said an Agusta AW139 helicopter from the Northern Territory Air Base in Bertam, Penang, was mobilised to carry out water-bombing operations in areas inaccessible to ground teams. He added that water from fire hydrants is also being used for firefighting operations in residential areas.

items.title: MACC: Lapses in oversight enable NGO fund misuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Investigations by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) have found that some non-governmental organisations (NGOs) collecting public donations have deviated from their original objectives, including using funds to amass extraordinary wealth and purchase luxury items. Many were found to have misused funds for personal gain, involving not only top management but also members and certain individuals within the organisations. These groups typically exploit loopholes in the NGO oversight system to generate easy wealth, given the large sums collected from donors.

items.title: Billionaire Elon Musk enters courtroom showdown with OpenAI
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: Jury selection is to begin Monday in a high-profile legal battle between billionaire Elon Musk and artificial intelligence startup OpenAI, which he accuses of betraying its non-profit mission. OpenAI's ChatGPT is a formidable rival to the Grok chatbot made by Musk's xAI lab. Musk duped?

items.title: What we know about new MACC chief Abdul Halim Aman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman, a former High Court judge with nearly two decades of judicial experience and a long career in the legal service, has been appointed the new Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner. He was elevated to High Court Judge in 2007. He later served at the Penang High Court (2009) and Johor Baru High Court (2010) before his long tenure at the Shah Alam High Court from 2013 until his mandatory retirement in March 2023.

items.title: Japanese fugitive murder suspect nabbed after seven years hiding in Malaysia [WATCH[
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Japanese fugitive wanted for murder in his home country has been deported after evading authorities for seven years, in a case that underscores Malaysia's growing role in global crime enforcement cooperation. Police confirmed the suspect, who had been on the Interpol Red Notice list, was tracked down and detained in Malaysia before being handed over to Japanese authorities. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the arrest was the result of close coordination between Malaysia and Japan, led by the police's Interpol division.

items.title: Azam: NGOs cannot take 30pct of public donations as commission
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has clarified that non-governmental organisations (NGOs) are not permitted to take 30 per cent of public donations as income or commission, describing such belief as incorrect. "This 30 per cent figure does not mean that NGOs or their founders can take 30 per cent of public donations as commission or profit. "The condition is 30 per cent of annual expenditure, not 30 per cent of total donations.

items.title: Humanitarian scam: NGOs allegedly profited from images of Palestinian war victims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Exploiting the suffering of war victims, some non-governmental organisations (NGOs) are believed to be using harrowing images of destruction to solicit public donations—only for the funds to be misappropriated for personal gain. Revealing these patterns, Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said investigations into such cases involve detailed scrutiny of financial flows, accounting records, transaction documents, and management decisions within the organisations. "In several cases reported previously, investigations found specific modus operandi used by irresponsible parties to misuse NGO funds.

items.title: US says two killed in boat strike as toll climbs over 180
items.summary: WASHINGTON, (United States): The US military said it killed two people in a strike on an alleged drug-trafficking vessel Friday, as the death toll of Washington's campaign against "narco-terrorists" in Latin America climbed to at least 182. The military "conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organisations," the US Southern Command, which is responsible for Washington's forces in the region, said in an X post. US military officials have claimed at least seven such strikes in April, bringing the total toll of people killed in these operations at least 182, according to an AFP tally.

items.title: HR Ministry: WFH productivity not a myth, 62pc of bosses agree
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 25 — The implementation of flexible working arrangements (FWA) is in line with the needs of an increasingly dynamic labour market that requires more adaptive, responsive and productivity-driven work models, according to the Ministry of Human Resources (KESUMA). The ministry, in a statement today, said the implementation of FWA – which includes hybrid work models, flexible working hours and arrangements – can enhance organisational efficiency while improving employee well-being. “It also, among others, gives employees the right to apply for flexible working arrangements and requires employers to provide a written response within a stipulated period,” the ministry said.

items.title: Japanese murder fugitive who posed as Myanmar national deported after years on the run
items.summary: The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) confirmed the deportation today, completing a complex international investigation that relied on forensic science, including a crucial DNA match, to unmask the wanted man. The man was initially arrested by the Kedah police’s Narcotic Criminal Investigation Department for drug and immigration offences. According to Criminal Investigation Department (CID) Director Commissioner Datuk M. Kumar, investigators used scientific and forensic methods to confirm the fugitive’s true identity.

items.title: Malaysia names new MACC chief ahead of planned rally in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — The Malaysian government today announced a new Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner, pre-empting a planned rally this evening to demand the removal of the incumbent. Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar this morning announced that the new MACC chief from May 13 onwards would be Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman. Shamsul Azri said the King had assented to Abdul Halim’s appointment as the new MACC chief, in line with Section 5(1) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009.

items.title: Anwar in Penang today for JPJ 80th anniversary, Seberang Perai development talks
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake a one-day working visit to Penang today to attend several key official programmes focusing on development and public well-being. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, is scheduled to attend the 80th anniversary celebration of the Road Transport Department (JPJ) and the JPJ MADANI Mega Carnival at the Penang State Stadium grounds in Batu Kawan at 10.30 am. Also accompanying him at the event are Transport Minister Anthony Loke, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abdul Bakar, and JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli.

items.title: Is your meat safe? Police seize over a ton of illegal pork in major Skudai bust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Police raided a frozen food distribution outlet in Skudai, Johor, today for allegedly storing pork carcasses without valid slaughter approval. The Internal Security and Public Order Department’s Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Intelligence Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander, Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar, said the 10.30 am raid found frozen pork carcasses with dubious slaughter documentation. Mohd Zaki said seven people were detained, including six Nepalese men and a local woman who owns the premises.

items.title: Anwar’s aide: Oil royalties should be used for projects, not political capital
items.summary: IPOH, April 25 — The federal government’s approach toward petroleum royalty payments to state governments should be viewed within the context of development project implementation and should not be used as cheap political capital by the opposition to gain public support. Political Secretary to the Minister of Finance, Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim, stated that this step is an existing mechanism involving coordination between the federal and state governments to ensure funds are utilised effectively. According to him, while KR1M involved large-scale use of government funds, MADANI Mart is implemented through collaboration between Yayasan MADANI and private parties interested in the retail business.

items.title: Jakim: State religious councils not involved in ‘zakat’ fraud allegations
items.summary: PADANG RENGAS, April 25 — Malaysian Islamic Development Department (Jakim) has urged the public to continue trusting the tithe institution and let authorities investigate recent viral allegations of zakat fund misappropriation. Its director-general, Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee, said the State Islamic Religious Councils are not involved in the issue. “The councils entrusted with managing zakat collection are not involved.

items.title: Two-year-old among four riding one motorcycle dies after Bukit Mertajam road crash
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, April 25 — A two-year-old boy was killed, while his mother was seriously injured, after the motorcycle they were riding with two others skidded and crashed into a stone wall along Jalan Song Ban Kheng in Bukit Mertajam yesterday. She was riding the motorcycle with her three children as passengers. “She sustained serious head injuries, while her two-year-old son was pronounced dead while receiving treatment at Bukit Mertajam Hospital due to head injuries,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Selangor MB: 168-acre Permatang Kumbang plot in Puchong hasn’t been a forest reserve since 1926
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 25 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari yesterday clarified that a 168-acre plot of land in Permatang Kumbang, Puchong city centre, is not a permanent forest reserve as claimed by irresponsible parties. Amirudin said the land, located near the Ayer Hitam Forest Reserve in Kinrara, was degazetted in 1926, approximately 100 years ago. Amirudin said his administration has never approved any development on the land.

items.title: In Sandakan, some fire survivors say being turned away from full relief centres
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 25 — Some victims of the Kampung Bahagia fire here are appealing to the authorities to open more relief centres, claiming they were unable to register as existing centres are already full and registration has been closed. One victim, Almahmud Muhamir, 37, said his application was rejected when he tried to register at the relief centre at the Batu Sapi People’s Housing Project Hall yesterday. “When I got there, I was told registration was no longer open.

items.title: Middle finger and road rage: Melaka police search for viral express bus driver
items.summary: MELAKA, April 25 — Police have urged a chartered bus driver, recorded on video allegedly behaving provocatively and making a rude hand gesture towards a car driver Thursday, to come forward to assist in investigations. Melaka Tengah district police chief ACP Christopher Patit said police detected the video at about 2 pm Thursday after it went viral on Facebook, where it was uploaded by an account under the name Muhazam Shah. “The victim, a 61-year-old local man driving a Proton Persona, lodged a police report on the incident at 11.38 am yesterday.

items.title: Two bikers arrested after Putrajaya bridge drag races spark public uproar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Two motorcyclists were arrested by the police for allegedly performing dangerous stunts along Putra Bridge in Putrajaya that went viral on Thursday. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said they identified one out of two rmotorcyclists performing dangerous stunts on high-powered motorcycles before the man, 31, showed up at the Putrajaya police headquarters’ traffic station and confessed to performing the stunts at 12.30 am April 19. The police also arrested another motorcyclist, 33, and seized two high-powered motorcycles believed to be used for the stunts, he said, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 81 of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Police probe brazen Ipoh cable theft after TikTok video goes viral
items.summary: IPOH, April 25 — Police are tracking down a group of individuals who went viral on social media and are believed to be involved in cable theft at Jalan Raja Musa Aziz here. Ipoh police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the video was uploaded by social activist Faiz under the username @faiz.sosialaktivis on TikTok. Police view this incident seriously as it could affect property, telecommunications services, and public safety,” he said in a statement.

items.title: RM2.3m for the car, RM0 for road tax: Lamborghini Revuelto ends up in Perlis JPJ impound
items.summary: ARAU, April 25 — The Perlis Road Transport Department (JPJ) has seized a Lamborghini Revuelto, estimated to be worth RM2.3 million, after discovering its road tax had expired and it had no insurance coverage. Its annual road tax is approximately RM17,873 per year in Peninsular Malaysia. The luxury vehicle was stopped during a routine operation along the Kuala Perlis-Changlun road recently.

items.title: Padini says some bank accounts frozen by MACC in anti-money laundering probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Several bank accounts belonging to fashion retailer Padini Holdings Berhad and its subsidiaries have been frozen by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in connection with an anti-money laundering investigation. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia yesterday, the company announced it was notified on April 24 that the freezing order had been issued under Section 44(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA). Operations not materially affected Padini moved quickly to reassure the market, stating that the freeze is not expected to have a material impact on its financial and daily operations.

items.title: An unlikely (and adorable) ally joins the fight to prevent MRT cable thefts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — In the fight against cable theft, an unlikely ally has emerged — trained sniffer dogs — that are now leading patrols across hard-to-reach areas where authorities once struggled to cover vast, hidden stretches of rail track. Despite persistent cable thefts along the combined 116km stretch of the Kajang and Putrajaya MRT lines, even with heightened security enforcement, these furry companions have proven indispensable. Power cables, including signal, communication and traction power cables, are targeted by thieves along rail tracks for their valuable metal content.

items.title: Malaysia’s fertility sector grows into regional medical tourism draw
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, April 25 — Malaysian actress, television host and wellness entrepreneur Sarah Lian was in her late 30s and still single when she started to think about fertility preservation treatments such as egg freezing. “Like many women today, I didn’t prioritise fertility in my 20s… it is not something you think about at that age. GenPrime Everlink Fertility Centre medical director Dr Mathi Arasu said Malaysia is considered a regional hub for many seeking IVF and other fertility treatments.

items.title: Supersonic jets, cardboard drones and blast-proof specs: The defence drops that turned heads at DSA 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — From a supersonic jet to cardboard drones, some of the world’s most cutting-edge defence tech landed in Kuala Lumpur this week, offering a rare look inside a fast-growing global industry. Currently in the testing phase, the jamming-resistant Hunterex-Dark and Hunterex-Fibre drones are designed for offensive covert operations. — Picture by Dhesegaan Bala Krishnan Japan’s cardboard combat drones Forget carbon fibre or titanium — one Japanese company presented military-grade drones made from cardboard.

items.title: Fahmi: Military housing broadband rollout delivers affordable high‑speed internet, several camps completed early
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH, April 24 — The Fibre-to-the-Home (FTTH) project for Armed Forces Family Housing (RKAT) nationwide is progressing encouragingly, with several locations completed well ahead of schedule, some by as much as 11 to 14 months. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the RKAT Broadband Project is a national initiative under a strategic collaboration between the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) and the Malaysian Armed Forces (ATM), formalised through a memorandum of understanding signed on October 11, 2024. The project involves more than 59,000 RKAT units across 73 military camps nationwide.

items.title: MACC: Former company director arrested, to be charged over RM200m loan fraud
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained a former director of a telecommunications company for allegedly submitting documents containing false particulars to a development financial institution to secure approval for a loan of about RM200 million. Meanwhile, MACC senior director (Special Operations) Datuk Mohamad Zamri Zainul Abidin, when contacted, confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009. He said the suspect will be charged this Monday, at a Kuala Lumpur Court.

items.title: Malaysia shifts from smart cities to ‘wise cities’ approach to balance growth with sustainability, says DPM Fadillah
items.summary: MELAKA, April 24 — Malaysia is shifting its urban development approach from smart cities to “wise cities” as it seeks to balance growth, sustainability and inclusivity in addressing increasingly complex urban challenges, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. Fadillah, who is also Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, said a “wise city” goes beyond the use of technology and instead emphasises sound judgement in managing development while safeguarding long-term environmental and social well-being. Fadillah said cities play a vital role in Malaysia’s commitment to achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2050, as urban systems are among the largest energy consumers.

items.title: Anwar says Malaysia Education Blueprint 2026–2035 designed to help schools adapt to AI and evolving demands
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 24 — The Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026-2035 is designed to ensure the national education system remains in step with evolving demands, particularly developments in artificial intelligence (AI), said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the initiative aims to ensure the Ministry of Education (MOE) has room to improve the education system with a forward-looking approach. Also present was Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek.

items.title: Nadma launches MyDIMS app for real‑time disaster alerts and reporting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — The National Disaster Management Agency (NADMA) has launched the Malaysian Disaster Information Management System (MyDIMS) application, which allows the public to monitor situations and report any disaster-related information. The launch of the application was officiated by NADMA director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim at the Resilient Asia 2026 exhibition here today. According to Meor Ismail, MyDIMS is an information application that integrates alert and disaster data from various technical agencies in a consolidated manner.

items.title: KTMB’s Madani30 code offers 30pc discount on ETS, ERT tickets until April 30
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 24 — The public is encouraged to take advantage of a 30 per cent discount on tickets for the Electric Train Service (ETS) and Ekspres Rakyat Timuran (ERT) before the purchase period ends on April 30. In statement today, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) said the offer is available using the promo code Madani30 via the KITS Style app, ticket kiosks or its official website. “By entering the code during purchase, users can enjoy savings on ETS services across all sectors, as well as ERT travel on the JB Sentral–Tumpat–JB Sentral route.
